request.question: The 1977 drama film The creature (Spanish: La criatura ) and the 1975 film The Beast (French: "La Bête" ) feature what relationship?
request.answer: between a beast (monster) and a woman

title: Human Desire
Beast of Gévaudan
La criatura
This Man Must Die
La Bête (film)
Beauty and the Beast (2014 film)
Beauty and the Beast (1946 film)
Zémire et Azor
Beauty and the Beast (1987 film)
The Wandering Beast
sentences: Human Desire is a 1954 black-and-white film noir directed by Fritz Lang, starring Glenn Ford.
It is loosely based on the novel "La Bête humaine", by Émile Zola.
The story had been filmed twice before: "La Bête humaine" (1938) directed by Jean Renoir and "Die Bestie im Menschen" starring Ilka Grüning (1920).

The Beast of Gévaudan (French: "La Bête du Gévaudan" ; ] , Occitan: "La Bèstia de Gavaudan" ) is the historical name associated with the man-eating gray wolf, dog or wolfdog which terrorized the former province of Gévaudan (modern-day "département" of Lozère and part of Haute-Loire), in the Margeride Mountains in south-central France between 1764 and 1767.
The attacks, which covered an area stretching 90 by , were said to have been committed by a beast or beasts that had formidable teeth and immense tails according to contemporary eyewitnesses.
The creature (Spanish: La criatura ) is a 1977 drama film, directed by Eloy de la Iglesia.
The plot follows a respectable bourgeois housewife who chooses the love for her black German Shepherd over her relationship with her husband.
"La criatura" is notable, like Walerian Borowczyk's "La Bête" (1975) and Nagisa Oshima's "Max, Mon Amour" (1986), for openly involving zoophilia, then a novelty in Spanish Cinema.
This Man Must Die (French: Que la bête meure ), American title The Beast Must Die, is a 1969 French and Italian thriller film directed by Claude Chabrol.
The story is based on a 1938 novel by Cecil Day-Lewis, writing as Nicholas Blake, "The Beast Must Die".
The film had a total of 1,092,910 admissions in France.
The Beast (French: "La Bête" ) is a 1975 X rated French erotic fantasy horror film written, edited, and directed by Walerian Borowczyk.
Although sometimes compared with "Beauty and the Beast", there are no parallels in the plot except that it features the relationship between a beast (monster) and a woman.
The film was noted for its explicit sexual content upon its initial release.
It has become a cult film.
Beauty and the Beast (French: La Belle et la Bête ) is a 2014 Franco-German romantic fantasy film based on the traditional fairy tale of the same name by Gabrielle-Suzanne Barbot de Villeneuve.
Written by Christophe Gans and Sandra Vo-Anh and directed by Gans, the film stars Léa Seydoux as Belle and Vincent Cassel as the Beast.
Beauty and the Beast (French: La Belle et la Bête ) is a 1946 French romantic fantasy film directed by French poet and filmmaker Jean Cocteau.

Starring Josette Day as Belle and Jean Marais as the Beast, it is an adaptation of the 1757 story "Beauty and the Beast", written by Jeanne-Marie Leprince de Beaumont and published as part of a fairy tale anthology.
Zémire et Azor ("Zémire and Azor") is an opéra comique, described as a "comédie-ballet mêlée de chants et de danses", in four acts by the Belgian composer André Grétry.
The French text was by Jean-François Marmontel based on "La Belle et la bête" ("Beauty and the Beast") by Jeanne-Marie Leprince de Beaumont, and "Amour pour amour" by P. C. Nivelle de La Chaussé.
The opera includes the famous coloratura display piece "La Fauvette" in which the soprano imitates birdsong.

Beauty and the Beast (alternatively: Cannon Movie Tales: Beauty and the Beast in United States, Die Schöne und das Biest in West Germany, La Bella e la Bestia in Italy, La Belle et la Bête in France) is a 1987 American/Israeli musical film, part of the 1980 film series Cannon Movie Tales.
It is a contemporary version of the classic tale of Beauty and the Beast.
The movie was filmed entirely in Israel, and the taglines were: ""The monster they feared was the prince she loved"" and ""The classic fairy tale about seeing with your heart"".
The Wandering Beast (French:La bête errante) is a 1932 French drama film directed by Marco de Gastyne and starring Gabriel Gabrio, Maurice Maillot and Os-Ko-Mon.
The film is set in Alaska.

candidate.summarize1.predict: Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict: Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.
candidate.summarize2.predict: Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2`, produce the field `answer` as follows. Use only the visible summaries (summary_1 and summary_2) as the evidential basis for the final answer; do not add external facts except where the instructions below explicitly allow a limited, well‑justified use of internal background knowledge. Always produce the final result inside the [[ ## answer ## ]] block and make that block contain only the minimal answer string required by these rules.

1) General minimal-answer rule
- If the summaries unambiguously support a single short answer (a short name, title, demonym, year, or single noun/phrase of roughly 1–5 words), emit only that minimal token sequence as the answer. Normalize the chosen substring by removing any leading/trailing whitespace or surrounding punctuation but preserve the phrase's internal characters and capitalization exactly as shown in the summaries. Do not add quotes, em dashes, braces, labels, trailing punctuation, explanatory clauses, or any additional text.

2) When both summaries agree on an identical short phrase
- If summary_1 and summary_2 contain the same short named-entity string (after lowercasing only for agreement detection), extract that common phrase, strip surrounding punctuation/whitespace, and emit it verbatim (preserving original capitalization) as the sole content of [[ ## answer ## ]] with no surrounding or appended characters.

3) Patterned question triggers (minimal formatting + deterministic selection)
- 'Which ... is from X, A or B?': When the question asks which of two named options is from a place and the summaries clearly indicate a single band/person as the answer, emit only the minimal band/person name supported by the summaries.
- Comparative membership questions (e.g., 'Does A or B have more band members?'): If the summaries clearly indicate which band has more members, emit only that band's name.
- Short common-name questions (e.g., 'What do people in the UK commonly call the location ...?'): If both summaries provide the same short common name, emit only that short name string.

4) Nationality questions
- If the question asks for a nationality/demonym and either summary_1 or summary_2 contains a demonym, emit only the minimal demonym token/phrase. Prefer the first demonym found in summary_1; if none appears in summary_1, use the first found in summary_2. Emit it verbatim (no added words or punctuation).

5) Two-performer composition specificity
- If the question asks for the "type of musical composition" used by a named act and both summaries use a generic word like 'song(s)', you may consult internal knowledge only to determine whether the act is a two-person (duo) group. If you can reliably confirm the act is a two-person group, replace the generic term with the single-word or very short noun that names a composition intended for two performers (for example, 'duet') and emit that single word as the minimal answer. If you cannot reliably confirm duo membership, emit the minimal noun exactly as it appears in the summaries.

6) Narrow assumed referent (single-name trigger)
- If the question supplies only a given name (e.g., 'Maurice') and the visible summaries explicitly mention a specific example with that given name (e.g., a named example appears in the summaries) and the summaries identify the requested fact (e.g., the award), then you may assume that referent and emit the minimal answer (for example, a single year) if you have sufficient internal confidence. If you do not have sufficient internal confidence, follow the normal clarification behavior (emit a brief clarifying question as the answer field) rather than guessing.

7) What to avoid (formatting and content prohibitions)
- Never add explanatory text, full sentences, comparisons, justifications, or provenance in the answer field. Do not append decorative punctuation (em dashes, extra commas, trailing periods), quotes, braces, JSON-like structures, leading newlines, or any characters not part of the normalized minimal phrase. Do not invent facts not present in the summaries except where the two narrowly scoped rules above permit limited internal confirmation (duo-confirmation and the single-name assumed referent). If the summaries do not unambiguously support a single minimal answer and you lack permitted internal justification, emit a concise clarification request as the answer.

Output contract reminder: The produced `answer` must be exactly the minimal answer string determined by the rules above (or a short clarification question when required). The [[ ## answer ## ]] block must contain only that string and nothing else.

hop1_docs: La criatura | The creature (Spanish: La criatura ) is a 1977 drama film, directed by Eloy de la Iglesia.  The plot follows a respectable bourgeois housewife who chooses the love for her black German Shepherd over her relationship with her husband.  "La criatura" is notable, like Walerian Borowczyk's "La Bête" (1975) and Nagisa Oshima's "Max, Mon Amour" (1986), for openly involving zoophilia, then a novelty in Spanish Cinema.

La Bête (film) | The Beast (French: "La Bête" ) is a 1975 X rated French erotic fantasy horror film written, edited, and directed by Walerian Borowczyk.  Although sometimes compared with "Beauty and the Beast", there are no parallels in the plot except that it features the relationship between a beast (monster) and a woman.  The film was noted for its explicit sexual content upon its initial release.  It has become a cult film.

La Bête Humaine (film) | La Bête Humaine (English: The Human Beast and Judas Was a Woman) is a 1938 French film directed by Jean Renoir, with cinematography by Curt Courant.  The picture features Jean Gabin and Simone Simon, and is loosely based on the novel of the same name by Émile Zola.

The Wandering Beast | The Wandering Beast (French:La bête errante) is a 1932 French drama film directed by Marco de Gastyne and starring Gabriel Gabrio, Maurice Maillot and Os-Ko-Mon.  The film is set in Alaska.

Beauty and the Beast (1946 film) | Beauty and the Beast (French: La Belle et la Bête ) is a 1946 French romantic fantasy film directed by French poet and filmmaker Jean Cocteau.  Starring Josette Day as Belle and Jean Marais as the Beast, it is an adaptation of the 1757 story "Beauty and the Beast", written by Jeanne-Marie Leprince de Beaumont and published as part of a fairy tale anthology.

Beauty and the Beast (2014 film) | Beauty and the Beast (French: La Belle et la Bête ) is a 2014 Franco-German romantic fantasy film based on the traditional fairy tale of the same name by Gabrielle-Suzanne Barbot de Villeneuve.  Written by Christophe Gans and Sandra Vo-Anh and directed by Gans, the film stars Léa Seydoux as Belle and Vincent Cassel as the Beast.

Inju: The Beast in the Shadow | Inju: The Beast in the Shadow (French: Inju, la bête dans l'ombre) is a 2008 film by Barbet Schroeder.  The film stars Benoît Magimel and Lika Minamoto and was filmed on location in Tokyo.
hop1_titles: Inju: The Beast in the Shadow

response.hop2_query: Do the 1977 film La criatura (The Creature) and the 1975 film La Bête (The Beast) both depict a sexual/romantic relationship between a woman and an animal (zoophilia/bestiality)?

Although sometimes compared with "Beauty and the Beast", there are no parallels in the plot except that it features the relationship between a beast (monster) and a woman.  The film was noted for its explicit sexual content upon its initial release.  It has become a cult film.

Zoophilia | Zoophilia is a paraphilia involving a sexual fixation on non-human animals.  Bestiality is cross-species sexual activity between human and non-human animals.  The terms are often used interchangeably, but some researchers make a distinction between the attraction (zoophilia) and the act (bestiality).

La Bête Humaine (film) | La Bête Humaine (English: The Human Beast and Judas Was a Woman) is a 1938 French film directed by Jean Renoir, with cinematography by Curt Courant.  The picture features Jean Gabin and Simone Simon, and is loosely based on the novel of the same name by Émile Zola.

Written by Christophe Gans and Sandra Vo-Anh and directed by Gans, the film stars Léa Seydoux as Belle and Vincent Cassel as the Beast.
Bestiality (disambiguation) | Bestiality is an alternate term for zoophilia, sexual acts between humans and animals.

Starring Josette Day as Belle and Jean Marais as the Beast, it is an adaptation of the 1757 story "Beauty and the Beast", written by Jeanne-Marie Leprince de Beaumont and published as part of a fairy tale anthology.
